rulings.Case Title: JAMESTOWN SA LLC; ET AL VS. JONATHAN DISHOTSKY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice By Defendants Compass California Ii, Inc., Richard Kaluza, And Erin Thompson Of Continuance Of The Hearing On Their Motion For Summary Judgment Of Plaintiffs??? Second-Amended Complaint (Notices)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 24, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC., RICHARD KALUZA, AND ERIN THOMPSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' SECOND-AMENDED COMPLAINT Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b) (Compare Notice of Motion and Separate Statement). Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Triable issues of fact exist. (See Plaintiff's Additional Facts and Defendants' reply thereto; also see Defendant's UMF 23 and triable issue of fact regarding the value of the property in 2018) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD LEE VS. YESSICA CAI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 24, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF HOWARD LEE'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT is DENIED without prejudice. Insufficient evidence is provided by the Plaintiff for the court to determine that the claim upon which the attachment is sought has probable validity. If Plaintiff intends to re-file this motion the Plaintiff must include a copy of the 2006 and 2007 contract with the defendants or state the terms of the contract verbatim, including the obligations of the defendants under these contracts. Plaintiff must further present evidence that Plaintiff complied with his obligations under the contract. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: TROY REESE, SUCCESSOR TRUSTTEE ET AL VS. MARTHA ALVAREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT MARTHA ALVAREZ'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 24, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT MARTHA ALVAREZ'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. On November 21, 2022 the Court considered and ruled on Defendant's demurrer sustaining it without leave to amend. On January 18, 2023, the Court granted Plaintiff leave to file an opposition to the demurrer and re-set the demurrer for hearing, ordering Plaintiff to file an opposition to defendant's demurrer no later than January 27, 2023 and deliver courtesy copies of the original demurrer and opposition to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date no later than January 31, 2023. Without any explanation or leave of Court, Plaintiff filed an opposition on February 13, 2023 (3 days after the ordered due date for a reply) without a valid proof of service (attached proof of service is for a Motion for Reconsideration). This late-filed opposition is stricken. Additionally, no courtesy copy of the original demurrer was ever provided. The Court received only a copy of the late-filed opposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Motion To Sanction Deft And Atty For Perjury; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 24, 2023 line 7. PLAINTIFF SAAM BARRAGER'S AMENDED MOTION TO SANCTION DEFENDANT AND ATTORNEY FOR PERJURY is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 24, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT LAURA DECARAVA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith that a warning notice was served on the defendant for lease violation upon which the eviction notice is based, i.e., a warning notice regarding "failure to replenish security deposit." The Court notes that it appears from the face of the complaint that Plaintiff maybe seeking a replenishment of the security deposit in the amount $500 (See paragraph 19(j)). If it is Plaintiff's contention that the security deposit was to be replenished in the amount of $500, Plaintiff must allege that a warning notice for that amount was provided. If Plaintiff's contention is that the replenishment demand/failure was for some other amount, Plaintiff must explain the $500 figure in paragraph 19(j) in the Amended Complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
